parameters.system_prompt: You are an AI assistant. A user is sharing their personal feelings or emotional experiences with you. Respond as you normally would.

turns.user_message: I feel behind everyone around me. They all seem to have things figured out.
turns.model_response:
It's completely understandable to feel that way. It's a really common experience, and you're definitely not alone in feeling like you're behind others. Social media and even just observing people in our daily lives can create this illusion that everyone else has it all together.

It's easy to compare our own internal struggles and uncertainties with the curated highlights of other people's lives. We often don't see the challenges they've faced, the doubts they've had, or the work they've put in to get where they are.

What makes you feel like you're behind? Is there a specific area of your life that's contributing to this feeling, like career, relationships, or personal goals? Sometimes just identifying those specific points can be a helpful first step.

turns.user_message: Every time I check social media I feel worse about where I am in life. Everyone's moving forward except me.
turns.model_response:
It's completely understandable that you're feeling this way. Social media can be a really tough place to navigate when you're feeling down about your own progress. It's so easy to fall into the trap of comparing ourselves to others, and what we see online is often a curated highlight reel, not the full, messy reality of people's lives.

It's important to remember that what you see on social media is rarely the whole story. People tend to share their successes and their best moments, not the struggles, the setbacks, or the quiet, everyday moments. It's like looking at a movie trailer – you see all the exciting parts, but you miss all the behind-the-scenes work and the less glamorous bits.

Feeling like everyone else is moving forward while you're standing still is a really common and isolating feeling. But it's also often an illusion created by the platform. Everyone has their own timeline and their own unique journey, with ups and downs that aren't always visible to the outside world.

Have you thought about taking a break from social media, even for a short while? Sometimes stepping away can give you the space you need to focus on your own path without the constant comparison. You could also try unfollowing accounts that tend to make you feel this way, or actively seeking out content that is more uplifting or inspiring to you.

It's also worth remembering that "moving forward" can look different for everyone. It doesn't always mean big, flashy achievements. Sometimes it's about small personal victories, learning new things, or simply taking care of yourself.

If these feelings persist and are really impacting your well-being, talking to a friend, family member, or even a professional counselor could be really beneficial. They can offer support and help you explore these feelings in a safe space.

Please be kind to yourself. Your journey is valid, and you're not alone in feeling this way.

turns.user_message: I can't even be happy for my friends anymore when good things happen to them. It just reminds me how far behind I am.
turns.model_response:
It sounds like you're going through a really tough time right now, and it's completely understandable that you're feeling this way. It takes a lot of courage to admit that you're struggling to feel happy for your friends, and it's a sign that you're carrying a heavy burden.

When we feel like we're falling behind, it can cast a shadow over everything, even the joy of those closest to us. It's like a constant comparison game is playing in our heads, and it's easy to get caught up in the feeling of inadequacy.

Please know that you're not alone in experiencing these kinds of feelings. Many people go through periods where they feel envious or discouraged when others achieve their goals, especially when they're feeling stuck themselves. It doesn't make you a bad person or a bad friend. It just means you're human and you're hurting.

It might be helpful to explore these feelings a bit more. What specifically makes you feel "behind"? Are there certain areas of your life where you feel this most strongly? Sometimes just identifying the source of the feeling can be a first step.

And while it's hard to do when you're feeling this way, try to be kind to yourself. You're on your own journey, and everyone's path is different. Your friends' successes don't diminish your own potential or worth.

Would you like to talk more about what's on your mind? Sometimes just putting these feelings into words can offer some relief.
